facts.fact: The policy form number is 9611A ME (09/16).
The policy version is 2.0.
The policy is a Maine Auto Policy issued by Progressive Auto.
In return for the insured's payment of the premium, the insurer agrees to insure the insured subject to all the terms, conditions and limitations of the policy.
The insurer will insure the insured for the coverages and the limits of liability shown on the policy's declarations page.
The policy consists of the policy contract, the insurance application, the declarations page, and all endorsements to the policy.
Defined terms are printed in boldface type throughout the policy.
Defined terms have the same meaning whether in the singular, plural, or any other form.
'Additional auto' means an auto the insured becomes the owner of during the policy period that does not permanently replace an auto shown on the declarations page.
For a vehicle to qualify as an 'additional auto,' the insurer must insure all other autos the insured owns.
For a vehicle to qualify as an 'additional auto,' the additional auto must not be covered by any other insurance policy.
For a vehicle to qualify as an 'additional auto,' the insured must notify the insurer within 30 days of becoming the owner of the additional auto.
For a vehicle to qualify as an 'additional auto,' the insured must pay any additional premium due.
An additional auto will have the broadest coverage the insurer provides for any auto shown on the declarations page.
If the insured asks the insurer to insure an additional auto more than 30 days after becoming the owner, any coverage provided will begin at the time the insured requests coverage.
'Auto' means a land motor vehicle of the private passenger, pickup body, or cargo van type.
'Auto' means a land motor vehicle designed for operation principally upon public roads.
'Auto' means a land motor vehicle with at least four wheels.
'Auto' means a land motor vehicle with a gross vehicle weight rating of 12,000 pounds or less, according to the manufacturer's specifications.
'Auto' does not include step-vans.
'Auto' does not include parcel delivery vans.
'Auto' does not include cargo cutaway vans or other vans with cabs separate from the cargo area.
'Auto business' means the business of selling, leasing, repairing, parking, storing, servicing, delivering or testing vehicles.
'Bodily injury' means bodily harm, sickness, or disease, including death that results from bodily harm, sickness, or disease.
'Covered auto' means any auto or trailer shown on the declarations page for the coverages applicable to that auto or trailer.
'Covered auto' means any additional auto.
'Covered auto' means any replacement auto.
'Covered auto' means a trailer owned by the insured.
'Declarations page' means the document showing the insured's coverages, limits of liability, covered autos, premium, and other policy-related information.
The declarations page may also be referred to as the Auto Insurance Coverage Summary.
'Occupying' means in, on, entering or exiting.

'Personal vehicle sharing program' means a system or process, operated by a business, organization, network, group, or individual, that facilitates the sharing of private passenger motor vehicles for use by individuals, businesses, or other entities.
'Property damage' means physical damage to, destruction of, or loss of use of, tangible property.
'Rated resident' means a person residing in the same household as the insured at the time of the loss who is not a relative.
A 'rated resident' must be listed in the 'Drivers and household residents' section on the declarations page.
A 'rated resident' must not be designated as either an 'Excluded' or a 'List Only' driver.
'Relative' means a person residing in the same household as the insured, and related to the insured by blood, marriage or adoption.
'Relative' includes a ward, stepchild, or foster child.
The insured's unmarried dependent children temporarily away from home will qualify as a relative if they intend to continue to reside in the insured's household.
'Replacement auto' means an auto that permanently replaces an auto shown on the declarations page.
A replacement auto will have the same coverage as the auto it replaces if the replacement auto is not covered by any other insurance policy.

If the auto being replaced had coverage under Part IV—Damage To A Vehicle, such coverage will apply to the replacement auto only during the first 30 days after the insured becomes the owner unless the insured notifies the insurer within that 30-day period to extend coverage beyond the initial 30 days.

If the auto being replaced did not have coverage under Part IV—Damage To A Vehicle, such coverage may be added, but the replacement auto will have no coverage under Part IV until the insured notifies the insurer of the replacement auto and asks to add the coverage.
'Ride-sharing activity' means the use of any vehicle to provide transportation of persons or property in connection with a transportation network company.
Ride-sharing activity begins from the time a user logs on to, or signs in to, any online-enabled application, software, website or system.
Ride-sharing activity ends at the time the user logs out of, or signs off of, any online-enabled application, software, website or system.
Ride-sharing activity applies whether or not the user has accepted any passenger(s) or delivery assignment.
Ride-sharing activity includes the time the user is on the way to pick up any passenger(s) or property, or is transporting any passenger(s) or property.
'Trailer' means a non-motorized trailer, including a farm wagon or farm implement, designed to be towed on public roads by an auto.
'Trailer' does not include a trailer being used for commercial purposes.
'Trailer' does not include a trailer being used as an office, store, or for display purposes.
'Trailer' does not include a trailer being used as a passenger conveyance.

'Transportation network company' means a corporation, partnership, sole proprietorship, or other entity that uses any online-enabled application, software, website or system to connect drivers with clients or passengers to facilitate and/or provide transportation or delivery services for compensation or a fee.
'We,' 'us' and 'our' mean the underwriting company providing the insurance, as shown on the declarations page.
'You' and 'your' mean a person shown as a named insured on the declarations page.
'You' and 'your' mean the spouse of a named insured if residing in the same household at the time of the loss.

Under Part I, if the insured pays the premium for liability coverage, the insurer will pay damages for bodily injury and property damage for which an insured person becomes legally responsible because of an accident.
Damages under Part I include prejudgment interest awarded against an insured person.
The insurer will settle or defend, at the insurer's option, any claim for damages covered by Part I.
Under Part I, 'insured person' means you, a relative, or a rated resident with respect to an accident arising out of the ownership, maintenance or use of an auto or a trailer.
Under Part I, 'insured person' means any person with respect to an accident arising out of that person's use of a covered auto with the permission of you, a relative, or a rated resident.
Under Part I, 'insured person' means any person or organization with respect only to vicarious liability for the acts or omissions of a person described above.
Under Part I, 'insured person' means any 'Additional Interest' shown on the declarations page with respect only to its liability for the acts or omissions of covered persons.
In addition to the limit of liability, the insurer will pay all expenses incurred in the settlement of any claim or defense of any lawsuit for an insured person.

In addition to the limit of liability, the insurer will pay interest accruing after entry of judgment, until the insurer has paid, offered to pay, or deposited in court, that portion of the judgment which does not exceed the insurer's limit of liability.
The additional payment of post-judgment interest does not apply if the insurer has not been given notice of suit or the opportunity to defend an insured person.
In addition to the limit of liability, the insurer will pay the premium on any appeal bond or attachment bond required in any lawsuit the insurer defends.
The insurer has no duty to purchase a bond in an amount exceeding its limit of liability.
The insurer has no duty to apply for or furnish appeal bonds or attachment bonds.
In addition to the limit of liability, the insurer will pay up to $250 for a bail bond required because of an accident resulting in bodily injury or property damage covered under Part I.
The insurer has no duty to apply for or furnish a bail bond.
In addition to the limit of liability, the insurer will pay reasonable expenses, including loss of earnings up to $200 per day, incurred at the insurer's request.

Part I coverage, including the duty to defend, will not apply for bodily injury or property damage arising out of a vehicle or trailer being used to carry persons or property for compensation or a fee.

Part I coverage will not apply for bodily injury or property damage arising out of a vehicle or trailer being used for retail or wholesale delivery, including the pickup, transport or delivery of magazines, newspapers, mail or food.
Part I coverage will not apply for bodily injury or property damage arising out of a vehicle or trailer being used for ride-sharing activity.
The exclusion for carrying persons or property for compensation, retail/wholesale delivery, and ride-sharing activity does not apply to shared-expense car pools.
Part I coverage will not apply to any liability assumed under any contract or agreement by you, a relative, or a rated resident.
Part I coverage will not apply to bodily injury to an employee of that insured person arising out of or within the course of employment.
The employee bodily injury exclusion does not apply to domestic employees if benefits are neither paid nor required to be provided under workers' compensation, disability benefits, or similar laws.

Part I coverage will not apply to bodily injury or property damage arising out of an accident involving any vehicle while being maintained or used by a person while employed or engaged in any auto business.
The auto business exclusion does not apply to you, a relative, a rated resident, or an agent or employee of you, a relative, or a rated resident, when using a covered auto.

Part I coverage will not apply to bodily injury or property damage resulting from or sustained during practice or preparation for any pre-arranged or organized racing, stunting, speed or demolition contest or activity.
Part I coverage will not apply to bodily injury or property damage resulting from or sustained during any driving activity conducted on a permanent or temporary racetrack or racecourse.
Part I coverage will not apply to bodily injury or property damage due to a nuclear reaction or radiation.
Part I coverage will not apply to bodily injury or property damage for which insurance is afforded under a nuclear energy liability insurance contract.

Part I coverage will not apply to bodily injury or property damage for which insurance would be afforded under a nuclear energy liability insurance contract but for its termination upon exhaustion of its limit of liability.
Part I coverage will not apply to any obligation for which the United States Government is liable under the Federal Tort Claims Act.

Part I coverage will not apply to bodily injury or property damage caused by an intentional act of that insured person, or at the direction of that insured person, even if the actual injury or damage is different than that which was intended or expected.
Part I coverage will not apply to property damage to any property owned by, rented to, being transported by, used by, or in the charge of that insured person.
The property damage exclusion does not apply to a rented residence or a rented garage.

The property damage exclusion does not apply to reasonable rental costs actually expended by the owner of an auto if the auto was rented to and damaged by an insured person, provided such rental costs are not covered under Part IV, subject to a maximum of 45 days.

Part I coverage will not apply to bodily injury or property damage arising out of the ownership, maintenance or use of any vehicle owned by the insured or furnished or available for the insured's regular use, other than a covered auto for which this coverage has been purchased.

Part I coverage will not apply to bodily injury or property damage arising out of the ownership, maintenance or use of any vehicle owned by a relative or a rated resident or furnished or available for the regular use of a relative or a rated resident, other than a covered auto for which this coverage has been purchased.
The exclusion for vehicles owned by or available for regular use of a relative or rated resident does not apply to the insured's maintenance or use of such vehicle.

Part I coverage will not apply to bodily injury or property damage arising out of the use of a vehicle, other than a covered auto, without the permission of the owner of the vehicle or the person in lawful possession of the vehicle.

Part I coverage will not apply to bodily injury or property damage arising out of the use of a covered auto while leased or rented to others or given in exchange for any compensation, including while being used in connection with a personal vehicle sharing program.
The leased/rented to others exclusion does not apply to the operation of a covered auto by you, a relative, or a rated resident.
Part I coverage will not apply to punitive or exemplary damages.
Part I coverage will not apply to bodily injury or property damage caused by, or reasonably expected to result from, a criminal act or omission of that insured person.
For the criminal act exclusion in Part I to apply, the insured person must be convicted of or plead guilty to the criminal act or omission.
Criminal acts or omissions in Part I do not include traffic violations.
The limit of liability shown on the declarations page for liability coverage is the most the insurer will pay regardless of the number of claims made.
The limit of liability shown on the declarations page for liability coverage is the most the insurer will pay regardless of the number of covered autos.
The limit of liability shown on the declarations page for liability coverage is the most the insurer will pay regardless of the number of insured persons.
The limit of liability shown on the declarations page for liability coverage is the most the insurer will pay regardless of the number of lawsuits brought.
The limit of liability shown on the declarations page for liability coverage is the most the insurer will pay regardless of the number of vehicles involved in the accident.
The limit of liability shown on the declarations page for liability coverage is the most the insurer will pay regardless of the number of premiums paid.
If the declarations page shows a split limit, the amount shown for 'each person' is the most the insurer will pay for all damages due to bodily injury to one person resulting from any one accident.
Subject to the 'each person' limit, the amount shown for 'each accident' is the most the insurer will pay for all damages due to bodily injury sustained by two or more persons in any one accident.
The amount shown for 'property damage' in a split limit is the most the insurer will pay for the total of all property damage resulting from any one accident.

The 'each person' limit of liability applies to the total of all claims made for bodily injury to a person and all claims of others derived from such bodily injury, including emotional injury or mental anguish, loss of society, loss of companionship, loss of services, loss of consortium, and wrongful death.
If the declarations page shows that 'combined single limit' or 'CSL' applies, the amount shown is the most the insurer will pay for the total of all damages resulting from any one accident.
Without changing the combined single limit of liability, the insurer will comply with any law that requires it to provide any separate limits.
No one is entitled to duplicate payments for the same elements of damages under Part I.
Any payment to a person under Part I will be reduced by any payment to that person under Part III—Uninsured/Underinsured Motorist Coverage.
The insurer will not pay under Part I any expenses paid or payable under Part II—Medical Payments Coverage.
If multiple auto policies issued by the insurer are in effect for the insured, the insurer will pay no more than the highest limit of liability for this coverage available under any one policy.
An auto and attached trailer are considered one auto for Part I purposes.
The limits of liability will not be increased for an accident involving an auto that has an attached trailer.
When the insurer certifies the policy as proof of financial responsibility, the policy will comply with the law to the extent required.
The insured person must reimburse the insurer if the insurer makes a payment that it would not have made if the policy was not certified as proof of financial responsibility.
If there is any other applicable liability insurance or bond, the insurer's liability insurance will be excess over any other applicable liability insurance or bond.
If more than one liability insurance policy or bond applies on an excess basis, the insurer will pay only its share of the damages.
The insurer's share of damages under Other Insurance in Part I is the proportion that its limit of liability bears to the total of all applicable limits.

If an accident occurs in any state, territory or possession of the United States or any province or territory of Canada other than where the covered auto is principally garaged, and that jurisdiction has higher financial responsibility limits than shown on the declarations page, the policy will provide the higher limits.

If a jurisdiction requires a non-resident to maintain insurance, the policy will provide the greater of the required minimum amounts and types of coverage, or the limits of liability under the policy.

Under Part II, if the insured pays the premium for Medical Payments Coverage, the insurer will pay the reasonable expenses incurred for necessary medical services received within three years from the date of a motor vehicle accident because of bodily injury sustained by an insured person and caused by that motor vehicle accident.
The insurer, or someone on the insurer's behalf, will determine whether the expenses for medical services are reasonable.
The insurer, or someone on the insurer's behalf, will determine whether the medical services are necessary.
Under Part II, 'insured person' means you, a relative, or a rated resident while occupying an auto.
Under Part II, 'insured person' means you, a relative, or a rated resident when struck by a motor vehicle or a trailer while not occupying a self-propelled motorized vehicle.
Under Part II, 'insured person' means any other person while occupying a covered auto with the permission of you, a relative, or a rated resident.
Under Part II, 'medical services' means medical, surgical, dental, x-ray, ambulance, hospital, professional nursing, and funeral services.
Under Part II, 'medical services' includes the cost of eyeglasses, hearing aids, pharmaceuticals, orthopedics, and prosthetic devices.
Under Part II, 'motor vehicle' means a land motor vehicle designed for use principally on public roads.
Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury sustained by any person while occupying a covered auto while it is being used to carry persons or property for compensation or a fee.

Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury sustained by any person while occupying a covered auto while it is being used for retail or wholesale delivery, including the pickup, transport or delivery of magazines, newspapers, mail or food.
Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury sustained by any person while occupying a covered auto while it is being used for ride-sharing activity.
The exclusion for compensation, retail/wholesale delivery, and ride-sharing activity in Part II does not apply to shared-expense car pools.
Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury arising out of an accident involving a vehicle while being maintained or used by a person while employed or engaged in any auto business.
The auto business exclusion in Part II does not apply to you, a relative, a rated resident, or an agent or employee of you, a relative, or a rated resident, when using a covered auto.

Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury resulting from or sustained during practice or preparation for any pre-arranged or organized racing, stunting, speed or demolition contest or activity.
Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury resulting from or sustained during any driving activity conducted on a permanent or temporary racetrack or racecourse.
Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury due to a nuclear reaction or radiation.
Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury for which insurance is afforded under a nuclear energy liability insurance contract.

Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury for which insurance would be afforded under a nuclear energy liability insurance contract but for its termination upon exhaustion of its limit of liability.
Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury for which the United States Government is liable under the Federal Tort Claims Act.
Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury sustained by any person while occupying any vehicle or trailer while located for use as a residence or premises.
Part II coverage will not apply if workers' compensation benefits are available for the bodily injury.

Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury sustained by any person while occupying or when struck by any vehicle owned by the insured or furnished or available for the insured's regular use, other than a covered auto for which this coverage has been purchased.

Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury sustained by any person while occupying or when struck by any vehicle owned by a relative or a rated resident or furnished or available for the regular use of a relative or a rated resident, other than a covered auto for which this coverage has been purchased.
The exclusion in Part II for vehicles owned by or available for regular use of a relative or rated resident does not apply to the insured.

Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury to you, a relative, or a rated resident, while occupying any vehicle, other than a covered auto, without the permission of the owner of the vehicle or the person in lawful possession of the vehicle.

Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury to any person while occupying a covered auto while leased or rented to others or given in exchange for any compensation, including while being used in connection with a personal vehicle sharing program.
The leased/rented to others exclusion in Part II does not apply to the operation of a covered auto by you, a relative, or a rated resident.
Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury caused directly or indirectly by war (declared or undeclared) or civil war.

Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury caused directly or indirectly by warlike action by any military force of any government, sovereign or other authority using military personnel or agents, including any action taken to hinder or defend against an actual or expected attack.

Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury caused directly or indirectly by insurrection, rebellion, revolution, usurped power, or any action taken by a governmental authority to hinder or defend against any of these acts.
Part II coverage will not apply to bodily injury caused by, or reasonably expected to result from, a criminal act or omission of an insured person.
For the criminal act exclusion in Part II to apply, the insured person must be convicted of or plead guilty to the criminal act or omission.
Criminal acts or omissions in Part II do not include traffic violations.
The limit of liability shown on the declarations page for Medical Payments Coverage is the most the insurer will pay for each insured person injured in any one accident.
The Medical Payments limit applies regardless of the number of claims made, covered autos, insured persons, lawsuits brought, vehicles involved in the accident, or premiums paid.
No one will be entitled to duplicate payments under the policy for the same elements of damages under Part II.

Any amount payable to an insured person under Part II will be reduced by any amount paid or payable for the same expense under Part I—Liability To Others or Part III—Uninsured/Underinsured Motorist Coverage.

If multiple auto policies issued by the insurer are in effect for the insured, the insurer will pay no more than the highest limit of liability for Medical Payments Coverage available under any one policy.
If an insured person incurs expenses for medical services that the insurer deems to be unreasonable or unnecessary, the insurer may refuse to pay for those expenses and contest them.

If the medical service provider sues the insured person because the insurer refuses to pay for medical services deemed unreasonable or unnecessary, the insurer will pay any resulting defense costs, subject to the limit of liability for this coverage.

If the medical service provider sues the insured person because the insurer refuses to pay for medical services deemed unreasonable or unnecessary, the insurer will pay any resulting judgment against the insured person, subject to the limit of liability for this coverage.
The insurer will choose the counsel in the event a medical service provider sues the insured person over disputed medical expenses.
The insurer will also pay reasonable expenses, including loss of earnings up to $200 per day, incurred at the insurer's request in connection with contested medical expenses.

The insured person may not sue the insurer for expenses for medical services the insurer deems unreasonable or unnecessary unless the insured person paid the entire disputed amount to the medical service provider or the medical service provider has initiated collection activity against the insured person for the unreasonable or unnecessary expenses.
If there is other applicable auto medical payments insurance, the insurer will pay only its share of the loss.
The insurer's share of other applicable auto medical payments insurance is the proportion that its limit of liability bears to the total of all applicable limits.

Any insurance the insurer provides for an insured person occupying a vehicle or trailer, other than a covered auto, will be excess over any other auto insurance providing payments for medical services.

Under Part III, if the insured pays the premium for this coverage, the insurer will pay for damages that an insured person is legally entitled to recover from the owner or operator of an uninsured motor vehicle because of bodily injury sustained by an insured person, caused by an accident, and arising out of the ownership, maintenance or use of an uninsured motor vehicle.
The insurer will pay under Part III only after the limits of liability under all applicable bodily injury liability bonds and policies have been exhausted by payment of judgments or settlements.

Any judgment or settlement for damages against an owner or operator of an uninsured motor vehicle that arises out of a lawsuit brought without the insurer's written consent is not binding on the insurer.
Under Part III, 'insured person' means you, a relative, or a rated resident.
Under Part III, 'insured person' means any person while operating a covered auto with the permission of you, a relative, or a rated resident.
Under Part III, 'insured person' means any person occupying, but not operating, a covered auto.
Under Part III, 'insured person' means any person who is entitled to recover damages covered by this Part III because of bodily injury sustained by a covered person.
Under Part III, 'uninsured motor vehicle' means a land motor vehicle or trailer of any type to which no bodily injury liability bond or policy applies at the time of the accident.

Under Part III, 'uninsured motor vehicle' means a vehicle to which a bodily injury liability bond or policy applies at the time of the accident, but the bonding or insuring company denies coverage or is or becomes insolvent.

Under Part III, 'uninsured motor vehicle' means a vehicle to which a bodily injury liability bond or policy applies at the time of the accident, but its limit of liability for bodily injury is less than the minimum limit of liability for bodily injury specified by the financial responsibility law of the state in which the covered auto is principally garaged.

Under Part III, 'uninsured motor vehicle' means a vehicle whose owner or operator cannot be identified and which causes an accident resulting in bodily injury to an insured person, provided that the insured person, or someone on his or her behalf, reports the accident to the police or civil authority within 24 hours or as soon as practicable after the accident.

Under Part III, 'uninsured motor vehicle' means a vehicle to which a bodily injury liability bond or policy applies at the time of the accident, but the sum of all applicable limits of liability for bodily injury is less than the combined sum of the applicable coverage limit for Uninsured/Underinsured Motorist Coverage shown on the declarations page and the coverage limits available under all other applicable uninsured or underinsured motorist coverages.

An 'uninsured motor vehicle' does not include any vehicle or equipment owned by you, a relative, or a rated resident or furnished or available for the regular use of you, a relative, or a rated resident.
An 'uninsured motor vehicle' does not include any vehicle or equipment owned or operated by a self-insurer under any applicable motor vehicle law, except a self-insurer that is or becomes insolvent.
An 'uninsured motor vehicle' does not include any vehicle or equipment operated on rails or crawler treads.
An 'uninsured motor vehicle' does not include any vehicle or equipment designed mainly for use off public roads, while not on public roads.
An 'uninsured motor vehicle' does not include any vehicle or equipment while located for use as a residence or premises.
An 'uninsured motor vehicle' does not include any vehicle or equipment that is a covered auto.
Part III coverage will not apply to bodily injury sustained by any person while using or occupying a covered auto being used to carry persons or property for compensation or a fee.

Part III coverage will not apply to bodily injury sustained by any person while using or occupying a covered auto being used for retail or wholesale delivery, including the pickup, transport or delivery of magazines, newspapers, mail or food.
Part III coverage will not apply to bodily injury sustained by any person while using or occupying a covered auto being used for ride-sharing activity.
The exclusion for compensation, retail/wholesale delivery, and ride-sharing activity in Part III does not apply to shared-expense car pools.

Part III coverage will not apply to bodily injury sustained by any person while using or occupying a motor vehicle that is owned by or available for the regular use of you, a relative, or a rated resident, unless it is a covered auto insured under this Part III.

Part III coverage will not apply to bodily injury sustained by you, a relative, or a rated resident while using any vehicle, other than a covered auto, without the permission of the owner of the vehicle or the person in lawful possession of the vehicle.
Part III coverage will not apply directly or indirectly to benefit any insurer or self-insurer under workers' compensation law.
Part III coverage will not apply directly or indirectly to benefit any insurer or self-insurer under disability benefits law.
Part III coverage will not apply to any punitive or exemplary damages.
Part III coverage will not apply to bodily injury sustained by any person if that person or the legal representative of that person settles without the insurer's written consent.
Part III coverage will not apply to bodily injury arising out of the use of a covered auto while being used in connection with a personal vehicle sharing program.
The personal vehicle sharing program exclusion in Part III does not apply to the operation of a covered auto by you, a relative, or a rated resident.

The limit of liability shown on the declarations page for Uninsured/Underinsured Motorist Coverage is the most the insurer will pay regardless of the number of claims made, covered autos, insured persons, lawsuits brought, vehicles involved in the accident, or premiums paid.
If the declarations page shows a split limit for Part III, the amount shown for 'each person' is the most the insurer will pay for all damages due to bodily injury to one person.

Subject to the 'each person' limit for Part III, the amount shown for 'each accident' is the most the insurer will pay for all damages due to bodily injury sustained by two or more persons in any one accident.

The 'each person' limit of liability in Part III includes the total of all claims made for bodily injury to an insured person and all claims of others derived from such bodily injury, including emotional injury or mental anguish, loss of society, loss of companionship, loss of services, loss of consortium, and wrongful death.

If the declarations page shows that 'combined single limit' or 'CSL' applies in Part III, the amount shown is the most the insurer will pay for the total of all damages resulting from any one accident.
Without changing the total limit of liability for Part III CSL, the insurer will comply with any law that requires it to provide any separate limits.

If there is no other applicable uninsured or underinsured motorist coverage, the limits of liability under Part III will be reduced by all sums paid because of bodily injury by or on behalf of any persons or organizations that may be legally responsible.
If there is no other applicable uninsured or underinsured motorist coverage, the limits of liability under Part III will be reduced by all sums paid under Part I—Liability To Others.

If there is no other applicable uninsured or underinsured motorist coverage, the limits of liability under Part III will be reduced by all sums paid or payable because of bodily injury under workers' compensation law or disability benefits law.

If there is other applicable uninsured or underinsured motorist coverage, the total of the applicable limits of liability available under all uninsured or underinsured motorist coverages combined will be reduced by all sums paid because of bodily injury by or on behalf of any persons or organizations that may be legally responsible.
If there is other applicable uninsured or underinsured motorist coverage, the total available limits will be reduced by all sums paid under Part I—Liability To Others.

If there is other applicable uninsured or underinsured motorist coverage, the total available limits will be reduced by all sums paid or payable because of bodily injury under workers' compensation law or disability benefits law.
The insurer will not pay under Part III any expenses paid or payable under Part II—Medical Payments Coverage.
No one will be entitled to duplicate payments for the same elements of damages under Part III.

If multiple auto policies issued by the insurer are in effect for the insured, the insurer will pay no more than the highest limit of liability for Uninsured/Underinsured Motorist Coverage available under any one policy.

If there is other applicable uninsured or underinsured motorist coverage, the insurer will pay only its share of the damages, which is the proportion that its limit of liability bears to the total of all available coverage limits.
Any insurance the insurer provides with respect to a vehicle that is not a covered auto will be excess over any other uninsured or underinsured motorist coverage under Part III.

If the insurer and an insured person cannot agree on the legal liability of the operator or owner of an uninsured motor vehicle, or the amount of the damages sustained by the insured person, this will be determined by arbitration if both parties mutually agree to arbitration prior to the expiration of the bodily injury statute of limitations in the state in which the accident occurred.
In the event of arbitration under Part III, each party will select an arbitrator.
The two arbitrators in Part III arbitration will select a third arbitrator.

If the two arbitrators cannot agree on a third arbitrator within 30 days, then on joint application by the insured person and the insurer, the third arbitrator will be appointed by a court having jurisdiction.
Each party will pay the costs and fees of its arbitrator and any other expenses it incurs in Part III arbitration.
The costs and fees of the third arbitrator in Part III arbitration will be shared equally.
Unless both parties agree otherwise, arbitration under Part III will take place in the county in which the insured person resides.
Local rules of procedure and evidence will apply in Part III arbitration.

A decision agreed to by two of the arbitrators will be binding with respect to the legal liability of the operator or owner of an uninsured motor vehicle and the amount of the damages sustained by the insured person.
The arbitrators will have no authority to award an amount in excess of the limit of liability in Part III arbitration.
The insurer and an insured person may agree to an alternate form of arbitration under Part III.

Under Part IV Collision Coverage, if the insured pays the premium, the insurer will pay for sudden, direct and accidental loss to a covered auto, including an attached trailer, or a non-owned auto, and its custom parts or equipment, resulting from collision.
Under Part IV Collision Coverage, the insurer will pay the reasonable cost to replace any child safety seat damaged in an accident to which this coverage applies.

Under Part IV Comprehensive Coverage, if the insured pays the premium, the insurer will pay for sudden, direct and accidental loss to a covered auto, including an attached trailer, or a non-owned auto, and its custom parts or equipment, that is not caused by collision.
Under Comprehensive Coverage, a loss not caused by collision includes contact with an animal (including a bird).
Under Comprehensive Coverage, a loss not caused by collision includes explosion or earthquake.
Under Comprehensive Coverage, a loss not caused by collision includes fire.
Under Comprehensive Coverage, a loss not caused by collision includes malicious mischief or vandalism.
Under Comprehensive Coverage, a loss not caused by collision includes missiles or falling objects.
Under Comprehensive Coverage, a loss not caused by collision includes riot or civil commotion.
Under Comprehensive Coverage, a loss not caused by collision includes theft or larceny.
Under Comprehensive Coverage, a loss not caused by collision includes windstorm, hail, water or flood.
Under Comprehensive Coverage, a loss not caused by collision includes breakage of glass not caused by collision.
Under Comprehensive Coverage, the insurer will pay for reasonable transportation expenses incurred by the insured if a covered auto is stolen.
Under Comprehensive Coverage, the insurer will pay for loss of use damages that the insured is legally liable to pay if a non-owned auto is stolen.
A combined maximum of $900, not exceeding $30 per day, will apply to the additional benefits for transportation expenses and loss of use damages under Comprehensive Coverage.
The additional benefit for transportation expenses under Comprehensive Coverage will not apply if the insured purchased Rental Reimbursement Coverage for the stolen covered auto.
Coverage for transportation expenses and loss of use damages under Comprehensive Coverage begins 48 hours after the insured reports the theft to the insurer.
Coverage for transportation expenses and loss of use damages ends when the auto has been recovered and returned to the insured or its owner.
Coverage for transportation expenses and loss of use damages ends when the auto has been recovered and repaired.
Coverage for transportation expenses and loss of use damages ends when the auto has been replaced.
Coverage for transportation expenses and loss of use damages ends 72 hours after the insurer makes an offer to settle the loss if the auto is deemed by the insurer to be a total loss.
If the loss is theft of a rented non-owned auto, coverage for loss of use damages ends 30 days after loss of use begins on the loss.
The insurer must receive written proof of transportation expenses and loss of use damages.
Additional Custom Parts or Equipment Coverage pays for sudden, direct and accidental loss to custom parts or equipment on a covered auto for which this coverage has been purchased.

Additional Custom Parts or Equipment Coverage applies only if the insured has purchased both Comprehensive Coverage and Collision Coverage for that covered auto and the loss is covered under one of those coverages.
Additional Custom Parts or Equipment Coverage applies in addition to any coverage automatically provided for custom parts or equipment under Comprehensive Coverage or Collision Coverage.

Under Rental Reimbursement Coverage, the insurer will reimburse rental charges incurred when the insured rents an auto from a rental agency or auto repair shop due to a loss to a covered auto for which Rental Reimbursement Coverage has been purchased.
Rental Reimbursement Coverage applies only if the insured has purchased both Comprehensive Coverage and Collision Coverage for that covered auto and the loss is covered under one of those coverages.
Additional fees or charges for insurance, damage waivers, optional equipment, fuel, or accessories are not covered under Rental Reimbursement Coverage.
Rental Reimbursement Coverage is limited to the each day limit shown on the declarations page for a maximum of 30 days.
If Rental Reimbursement Coverage applies, no other coverage under the policy for rental expenses will apply.
Rental charges under Rental Reimbursement Coverage will be reimbursed beginning when the covered auto cannot be driven due to a loss.

Rental charges will be reimbursed beginning when the covered auto can be driven but the insured delivers the covered auto to an auto repair shop or one of the insurer's Service Centers for repairs due to the loss.
Rental Reimbursement Coverage ends when the covered auto has been returned to the insured.
Rental Reimbursement Coverage ends when the covered auto has been repaired.
Rental Reimbursement Coverage ends when the covered auto has been replaced.
Rental Reimbursement Coverage ends 72 hours after the insurer makes an offer to settle the loss if the covered auto is deemed by the insurer to be a total loss.
Rental Reimbursement Coverage ends when the insured incurs 30 days worth of rental charges.
The insured must provide the insurer written proof of rental charges to be reimbursed under Rental Reimbursement Coverage.

Under Loan/Lease Payoff Coverage, if the insured pays the premium and the covered auto is deemed by the insurer to be a total loss, the insurer will pay, in addition to any amounts otherwise payable under Part IV, the difference between the actual cash value of the covered auto at the time of the total loss and any greater amount the owner of the covered auto is legally obligated to pay under a written loan or lease agreement.
Under Loan/Lease Payoff Coverage, the amount owed under the loan or lease is reduced by unpaid finance charges or refunds due to the owner for such charges.
Under Loan/Lease Payoff Coverage, the amount owed under the loan or lease is reduced by excess mileage charges or charges for wear and tear.
Under Loan/Lease Payoff Coverage, the amount owed under the loan or lease is reduced by charges for extended warranties or refunds due to the owner for extended warranties.
Under Loan/Lease Payoff Coverage, the amount owed under the loan or lease is reduced by charges for credit insurance or refunds due to the owner for credit insurance.
Under Loan/Lease Payoff Coverage, the amount owed under the loan or lease is reduced by past due payments and charges for past due payments.
Under Loan/Lease Payoff Coverage, the amount owed under the loan or lease is reduced by collection or repossession expenses.
The insurer's payment under Loan/Lease Payoff Coverage shall not exceed the limit of liability shown on the declarations page.
The limit of liability for Loan/Lease Payoff Coverage is a percentage of the actual cash value of the covered auto at the time of the loss.
Loan/Lease Payoff Coverage applies only if the insured has purchased both Comprehensive Coverage and Collision Coverage for that covered auto and the loss is covered under one of those coverages.
Pet Injury Coverage is available if the insured has purchased Collision coverage for at least one covered auto under the policy.

Under Pet Injury Coverage, if the insured's pet sustains injury while inside a covered auto or non-owned auto at the time of a loss covered under Collision or Comprehensive coverage, the insurer will provide up to $1,000 for reasonable and customary veterinary fees incurred by you, a relative, or a rated resident.

Under Pet Injury Coverage, if the insured's pet dies in, or as a direct result of, a covered loss, the insurer will provide a $1,000 death benefit less any payment made toward veterinary expenses for the pet.

In the event of a covered loss due to theft of a covered auto or non-owned auto, the insurer will provide the Pet Injury death benefit provided the pet is inside that auto at the time of the theft and the pet is not recovered.
Under Part IV, 'collision' means the upset of a vehicle or its impact with another vehicle or object.

Under Part IV, 'custom parts or equipment' means equipment, devices, accessories, enhancements and changes, other than those that are offered by the manufacturer specifically for that auto model, or that are installed by the auto dealership as part of the original sale of a new auto, that are permanently installed or attached and alter the appearance or performance of the auto.
Under Part IV, 'mechanical parts' means operational parts on a vehicle that wear out over time or have a finite useful life or duration typically shorter than the life of the vehicle as a whole.
Mechanical parts do not include external crash parts, wheels, paint, or windshields and other glass.

Under Part IV, 'non-owned auto' means an auto that is not owned by or furnished or available for the regular use of you, a relative, or a rated resident while in the custody of or being operated by you, a relative, or a rated resident with the permission of the owner of the auto or the person in lawful possession of the auto.
Under Part IV, 'your pet' means any dog or cat owned by you, a relative, or a rated resident.
Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle while being used to carry persons or property for compensation or a fee.
Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle while being used for retail or wholesale delivery, including the pickup, transport or delivery of magazines, newspapers, mail or food.
Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle while being used for ride-sharing activity.
The exclusion for compensation, retail/wholesale delivery, and ride-sharing activity in Part IV does not apply to shared-expense car pools.
Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to a non-owned auto while being maintained or used by a person while employed or engaged in any auto business.

Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle resulting from or sustained during practice or preparation for any pre-arranged or organized racing, stunting, speed or demolition contest or activity, or any driving activity conducted on a permanent or temporary racetrack or racecourse.
Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle for which insurance is afforded under a nuclear energy liability insurance contract.

Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle caused by an intentional act committed by or at the direction of you, a relative, a rated resident, or the owner of a non-owned auto, even if the actual damage is different than that which was intended or expected.

Where a loss to a covered auto is caused by a named insured, the intentional act exclusion in Part IV shall not apply to the extent of the legal interest of any other named insured who had no knowledge of or participation in the intentional act and did not engage in fraudulent conduct in connection with the loss.

Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to a covered auto while it is leased or rented to others or given in exchange for compensation, including while being used in connection with a personal vehicle sharing program.
The leased/rented to others exclusion in Part IV does not apply to the operation of a covered auto by you, a relative, or a rated resident.

Part IV coverage will not apply for loss due to destruction or confiscation by governmental or civil authorities of any vehicle because you, any relative, or any rated resident engaged in illegal activities.
Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle that is due and confined to wear and tear.
Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle that is due and confined to freezing.
Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle that is due and confined to mechanical, electrical or electronic breakdown or failure.
Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle that is due and confined to road damage to tires.
The exclusion for wear and tear, freezing, mechanical/electrical/electronic breakdown, and road damage to tires does not apply if the damage results from the theft of a vehicle.

Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to portable equipment, devices, accessories, and any other personal effects that are not permanently installed, including tapes, compact discs, cassettes, DVDs, and other recording or recorded media.
Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any case or other container designed for use in storing or carrying tapes, compact discs, cassettes, DVDs, or other recording or recorded media.
Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any device used for the detection or location of radar, laser, or other speed measuring equipment or its transmissions.

Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to CB radios, telephones, two-way mobile radios, DVD players, personal computers, personal digital assistants, or televisions that are not permanently installed.
Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle for diminution of value.
Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle caused directly or indirectly by war (declared or undeclared) or civil war.

Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle caused directly or indirectly by warlike action by any military force of any government, sovereign, or other authority using military personnel or agents.

Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle caused directly or indirectly by insurrection, rebellion, revolution, usurped power, or any action taken by a governmental authority to hinder or defend against any of these acts.

Part IV coverage will not apply for loss to any vehicle caused by, or reasonably expected to result from, a criminal act or omission of you, a relative, a rated resident, or the owner of a non-owned auto.
For the criminal act exclusion in Part IV to apply, the insured person must be convicted of or plead guilty to the criminal acts or omission.
Criminal acts or omissions in Part IV do not include traffic violations.

The limit of liability for loss to a covered auto, non-owned auto, or custom parts or equipment is the lowest of the actual cash value of the stolen or damaged property at the time of the loss reduced by the applicable deductible, the amount necessary to replace the stolen or damaged property reduced by the applicable deductible, the amount necessary to repair the damaged property to its pre-loss condition reduced by the applicable deductible, or the Stated Amount shown on the declarations page for that covered auto.
The most the insurer will pay for loss to custom parts or equipment is $1,000 unless the insured purchased Additional Custom Parts or Equipment Coverage ('ACPE').
If the insured purchased ACPE, the most the insurer will pay for custom parts or equipment is $1,000 plus the amount of ACPE purchased.
The most the insurer will pay for loss to a trailer is the limit of liability shown on the declarations page for that trailer.
If a trailer is not shown on the declarations page, the limit of liability for that trailer is $500.
If coverage applies to a non-owned auto, the insurer will provide the broadest coverage applicable to any covered auto shown on the declarations page.
If the insured has elected a Stated Amount for a covered auto, the Stated Amount is the most the insurer will pay for all loss to that covered auto, including its custom parts or equipment.

Coverage for custom parts or equipment will not cause the insurer's limit of liability for loss to an auto under Part IV to be increased to an amount in excess of the actual cash value of the auto, including its custom parts or equipment.

In determining the amount necessary to repair damaged property to its pre-loss condition, the amount to be paid by the insurer will not exceed the prevailing competitive labor rates charged in the area where the property is to be repaired and the cost of repair or replacement parts and equipment, as reasonably determined by the insurer.

Determination of the prevailing competitive labor rates charged will be based on the rate being accepted by a willing and able repair facility providing similar services in the area where the property is to be repaired.

The amount to be paid by the insurer will be based on the cost of repair or replacement parts and equipment which may be new, reconditioned, remanufactured or used, including original manufacturer parts or equipment and nonoriginal manufacturer parts or equipment.
To determine the amount necessary to repair or replace damaged property, the total cost of necessary repair or replacement may be reduced by unrepaired prior damage.
Unrepaired prior damage includes broken, cracked or missing parts; rust; dents; scrapes; gouges; and peeling paint.

The reduction for unrepaired prior damage is the cost of labor, parts and materials necessary to repair or replace damage, deterioration, defects, or wear and tear on exterior body parts, windshields and other glass, wheels, and paint, that existed prior to the accident and that is eliminated as a result of the repair or replacement of property damaged in the loss.
To determine the amount necessary to repair or replace damaged property, an adjustment may be made for betterment or depreciation and physical condition on batteries.
To determine the amount necessary to repair or replace damaged property, an adjustment may be made for betterment or depreciation and physical condition on tires.

To determine the amount necessary to repair or replace damaged property, an adjustment may be made for betterment or depreciation and physical condition on engines and transmissions, if the engine has greater than 80,000 miles.

To determine the amount necessary to repair or replace damaged property, an adjustment may be made for betterment or depreciation and physical condition on any other mechanical parts that are nonfunctioning or inoperative.
The insurer will not make an adjustment for the labor costs associated with the replacement or repair of batteries, tires, engines and transmissions, or nonfunctioning mechanical parts.
The actual cash value of a vehicle equals its replacement cost at the time the loss occurs, less the value of its physical depreciation as determined by standard business practices.
The insurer will not pay for loss of use of a rental vehicle for more than 30 days.
No deductible will apply to a loss to window glass when the glass is repaired instead of replaced.
Duplicate recovery for the same elements of damages is not permitted under Part IV.
The most the insurer will pay for all Pet Injury damages in any one loss is a total of $1,000 regardless of the number of dogs or cats involved.
If a pet dies in, or as a direct result of, a covered loss, the insurer will provide a death benefit of $1,000, less any payment made toward veterinary expenses for the pet.
No deductible shall apply to Pet Injury coverage.
The insurer may, at its option, pay for a loss in money, or repair or replace the damaged or stolen property under Part IV.
At the insurer's expense, the insurer may return any recovered stolen property to the insured or to the address shown on the declarations page, with payment for any damage resulting from the theft.
The insurer may keep all or part of the property at the agreed or appraised value.
The insurer may settle any loss with the insured or the owner or lienholder of the property.
Coverage under Part IV will not directly or indirectly benefit any carrier or other bailee for hire.
Payment under Part IV for a loss to a covered auto will be made according to the insured's interest and the interest of any lienholder shown on the declarations page or designated by the insured.
At the insurer's option, payment may be made to both the insured and lienholder jointly, or to either separately.
If the covered auto is not a total loss, the insurer may make payment to the insured and the repairer of the auto.

The lienholder's interest will not be protected where fraud, misrepresentation, material omission, or intentional damage resulting in a denial of coverage by the insurer has been committed by or at the direction of you or any person seeking coverage.
The lienholder's interest will not be protected where the loss is otherwise not covered under the terms of the policy.
If the policy is cancelled, nonrenewed or voided, the interest of any lienholder under the agreement will also terminate.

If other sources of recovery also cover a Part IV loss, the insurer will pay only its share of the loss, which is the proportion that its limit of liability bears to the total of all applicable limits.

Any insurance provided for a non-owned auto, or trailer not shown on the declarations page, will be excess over any other collectible source of recovery, including any coverage provided by the owner of the non-owned auto or trailer, any other applicable physical damage insurance, and any other source of recovery applicable to the loss.
If the insurer and the insured cannot agree on the amount of a Part IV loss, then either party may demand an appraisal of the loss.
Within 30 days of any demand for an appraisal under Part IV, each party shall appoint a competent appraiser and shall notify the other party of that appraiser's identity.
The appraisers will determine the amount of loss in Part IV appraisal.
If the appraisers fail to agree, the disagreement will be submitted to a qualified umpire chosen by the appraisers.

If the two appraisers are unable to agree upon an umpire within 15 days, either the insurer or the insured may request that a judge of a court of record, in the county where the insured resides, select an umpire.
The appraisers and umpire will determine the amount of loss in Part IV appraisal.
The amount of loss agreed to by both appraisers, or by one appraiser and the umpire, will be binding in Part IV appraisal.
The insured will pay its appraiser's fees and expenses in Part IV appraisal.
The insurer will pay its appraiser's fees and expenses in Part IV appraisal.
All other expenses of the appraisal, including payment of the umpire if one is selected, will be shared equally between the insurer and the insured.
Neither the insurer nor the insured waives any rights under the policy by agreeing to an appraisal.

Under Part V Roadside Assistance Coverage, if the insured pays the premium, the insurer will pay for its authorized service representative to provide towing of a covered disabled auto to the nearest qualified repair facility when necessary due to a covered emergency.
Under Part V Roadside Assistance Coverage, the insurer will pay for labor on a covered disabled auto at the place of disablement when necessary due to a covered emergency.
If a covered disabled auto is towed to any place other than the nearest qualified repair facility, the insured will be responsible for any additional charges incurred.
Under Part V, 'covered disabled auto' means a covered auto for which this coverage has been purchased that sustains a covered emergency.
Under Part V, 'covered emergency' means a disablement that is a result of mechanical or electrical breakdown.
Under Part V, 'covered emergency' includes battery failure.
Under Part V, 'covered emergency' includes insufficient supply of fuel, oil, water, or other fluid.
Under Part V, 'covered emergency' includes a flat tire.
Under Part V, 'covered emergency' includes accidental lock-out.
Under Part V, 'covered emergency' includes entrapment in snow, mud, water or sand within 100 feet of a road or highway.
Part V coverage will not apply to more than three covered emergencies for any single covered auto in a six-month period.
Part V coverage will not apply to the cost of purchasing parts, fluid, lubricants, fuel, or replacement keys, or the labor to make replacement keys.
Part V coverage will not apply to installation of products or material not related to the disablement.
Part V coverage will not apply to labor not related to the disablement.
Part V coverage will not apply to labor on a covered disabled auto for any time period in excess of 60 minutes per disablement.
Part V coverage will not apply to towing or storage related to impoundment, abandonment, illegal parking, or other violations of law.
Part V coverage will not apply to assistance with jacks, levelers, airbags or awnings.
Part V coverage will not apply to labor or repair work performed at a service station, garage, or repair shop.
Part V coverage will not apply to auto storage charges.

Part V coverage will not apply to disablement that occurs on roads not regularly maintained, sand beaches, open fields, or areas designated as not passable due to construction, weather, or earth movement.
Part V coverage will not apply to mounting or removing of snow tires or chains.
Part V coverage will not apply to tire repair.
Part V coverage will not apply to disablement that results from an intentional or willful act or action by you, a relative, or the operator of a covered disabled auto.
Part V coverage will not apply to any covered auto while being used in connection with ride-sharing activity.
Part V coverage will not apply to any covered auto while being used in connection with a personal vehicle sharing program.
Part V coverage will not apply to a trailer.

When service is rendered by an unauthorized service provider (one in the business of providing roadside assistance and towing services but not an authorized service representative), the insurer will pay only reasonable charges, as determined by the insurer, for towing of a covered disabled auto to the nearest qualified repair facility and labor on a covered disabled auto at the place of disablement which is necessary due to a covered emergency.
Any coverage provided under Part V for service rendered by an unauthorized service provider will be excess over any other collectible insurance or towing protection coverage.
For coverage to apply under the policy, the insured or the person seeking coverage must promptly report each accident or loss even if the insured or the person seeking coverage is not at fault.
The insured or the person seeking coverage must provide the insurer with all accident or loss information, including time, place, and how the accident or loss happened.
The insured or the person seeking coverage must obtain and provide the names and addresses of all persons involved in the accident or loss.
The insured or the person seeking coverage must obtain and provide the names and addresses of any witnesses.
The insured or the person seeking coverage must obtain and provide the license plate numbers of the vehicles involved.

If the insured or the person seeking coverage cannot identify the owner or operator of a vehicle involved in the accident, or if theft or vandalism has occurred, the insured or the person seeking coverage must notify the police within 24 hours or as soon as practicable.
A person seeking coverage must cooperate with the insurer in any matter concerning a claim or lawsuit.
A person seeking coverage must provide any written proof of loss the insurer may reasonably require.

A person seeking coverage must allow the insurer to take signed and recorded statements, including sworn statements and examinations under oath, which the insurer may conduct outside the presence of the insured or any other person seeking coverage.
A person seeking coverage must answer all reasonable questions the insurer may ask as often as the insurer may reasonably require.
A person seeking coverage must promptly call to notify the insurer about any claim or lawsuit and send any and all legal papers relating to the claim or suit.
A person seeking coverage must attend hearings and trials as the insurer requires.
A person seeking coverage must take reasonable steps after a loss to protect the covered auto, or any other vehicle for which coverage is sought, from further loss.
The insurer will pay reasonable expenses incurred in protecting the vehicle from further loss.
If failure to protect the vehicle from further loss results in further loss, any additional damages will not be covered under the policy.
A person seeking coverage must allow the insurer to have the damaged covered auto, or any other damaged vehicle for which coverage is sought, inspected and appraised before its repair or disposal.
A person seeking coverage must submit to medical examinations at the insurer's expense by doctors the insurer selects as often as the insurer may reasonably require.
A person seeking coverage must authorize the insurer to obtain medical and other records.
The policy applies only to accidents and losses occurring during the policy period shown on the declarations page.

The policy applies only to accidents and losses occurring within a state, territory or possession of the United States of America, or a province or territory of Canada, or while a covered auto is being transported between their ports.

The policy contract, the insurance application (which is made a part of the policy as if attached hereto), the declarations page, and all endorsements to the policy issued by the insurer contain all the agreements between the insured and the insurer.
The terms of the policy may not be changed or waived except by an endorsement issued by the insurer.
The premium for the policy is based on information the insurer received from the insured and other sources.
The insured agrees to cooperate with the insurer in determining if the premium information is correct and complete, and to promptly notify the insurer if it changes during the policy period.

If the information is determined by the insurer to be incorrect, incomplete, or if it changes during the policy period, the insured agrees that the insurer may adjust the policy information and premium accordingly.
Changes that may result in a premium adjustment include the insured, a relative, or a rated resident obtaining a driver's license or operator's permit.
Changes that may result in a premium adjustment include changes in the number, type or use classification of covered autos.
Changes that may result in a premium adjustment include changes in the persons who regularly operate a covered auto.
Changes that may result in a premium adjustment include changes in the persons of legal driving age residing in the insured's household.
Changes that may result in a premium adjustment include changes in the residents in the insured's household.
Changes that may result in a premium adjustment include changes in an operator's marital status.
Changes that may result in a premium adjustment include changes in the insured's mailing address and residence address.
Changes that may result in a premium adjustment include changes in the principal garaging address of any covered auto.
Changes that may result in a premium adjustment include changes in coverage, deductibles, or limits of liability.
Changes that may result in a premium adjustment include changes in rating territory or discount eligibility.
The coverage provided in the policy may be changed only by the issuance of a new policy or an endorsement by the insurer.

If during the policy period the insurer broadens any coverage afforded under the current edition of the policy without additional premium charge, that change will automatically apply to the policy as of the date the coverage change is implemented in Maine.
If the insured asks the insurer to delete a vehicle from the policy, no coverage will apply to that vehicle as of the date and time the insured asks to delete it.
The insured must promptly report to the insurer all changes, including additions and deletions, in policy information, including changes in the insured's mailing address or residence address.
The insured must report changes in the principal garaging address of any covered auto.
The insured must report changes in the residents in the insured's household.
The insured must report changes in the persons of legal driving age residing in the insured's household.
The insured must report changes in the persons who regularly operate a covered auto.
The insured must report changes in an operator's marital status.
The insured must report changes in the driver's license or operator's permit status of you, a relative, or a rated resident.

The insurer may use estimating, appraisal, or injury evaluation systems to assist in adjusting claims under the policy and to assist in determining the amount of damages, expenses, or loss payable under the policy.
Such systems may be developed by the insurer or a third party and may include computer software, databases, and specialized technology.
If any provision of the policy fails to conform to the statutes of the state listed on the insured's application as their residence, the provision shall be deemed amended to conform to such statutes.
All other provisions shall be given full force and effect if a provision is amended to conform to statutes.
Any disputes as to the coverages provided or the provisions of the policy shall be governed by the law of the state listed on the insured's application as the insured's residence.
The rights and duties under the policy may not be transferred to another person without the insurer's written consent.
If a named insured shown on the declarations page dies, the policy will provide coverage until the end of the policy period for the legal representative of the named insured, while acting as such.
If a named insured dies, the policy will provide coverage until the end of the policy period for persons covered under the policy on the date of the named insured's death.
Any changes made at the insured's request to the policy after inception will be made in reliance upon information the insured provides.

The insurer may deny coverage for an accident or loss if the insured makes incorrect statements or representations with regard to any material fact or circumstance in connection with any requested change or at any time during the policy period.
The insurer may deny coverage for an accident or loss if the insured conceals or misrepresents any material fact or circumstance.
The insurer may deny coverage for an accident or loss if the insured engages in fraudulent conduct, and that concealment, misrepresentation, or fraudulent conduct was material to a risk assumed.

The insurer may deny coverage for an accident or loss if the insured or a person seeking coverage has concealed or misrepresented any material fact or circumstance, or engaged in fraudulent conduct which materially affects the insurer's interests, in connection with the presentation or settlement of a claim.
In addition to premium, fees may be charged on the policy.
The insurer may charge fees for installment payments, late payments, and other transactions.

Fees may also be charged if the insured tenders a check, draft, electronic funds transfer, or other type of remittance and the method of such payment is returned to the insurer or refused because of insufficient funds, a closed account, or a stop payment order.
Payments made on the policy will be applied first to fees, then to premium due.
The insured may cancel the policy during the policy period by calling or writing the insurer and stating the future date the insured wishes the cancellation to be effective.

The insurer may cancel the policy during the policy period by mailing a notice of cancellation to the named insured shown on the declarations page at the last known address appearing in the insurer's records.
If the policy is cancelled for nonpayment of premium, the insurer will mail notice of cancellation to the insured at least 15 days before cancellation takes effect.
In all other cases, the insurer will mail notice of cancellation to the insured at least 25 days before the cancellation takes effect.
The insurer may cancel the policy for any reason if the notice is received by the named insured within the first 59 days of the initial policy period.
After the policy is in effect for more than 59 days, or if this is a renewal or continuation policy, the insurer may cancel only for nonpayment of premium.

After the policy is in effect for more than 59 days, or if this is a renewal or continuation policy, the insurer may cancel for material misrepresentation or fraud by the insured with respect to any material fact in the procurement, continuation, change or renewal of this policy.

After the policy is in effect for more than 59 days, or if this is a renewal or continuation policy, the insurer may cancel for material misrepresentation or fraud in the submission of any claim under this policy.

After the policy is in effect for more than 59 days, the insurer may cancel for loss of driving privileges through suspension, revocation or expiration of an operator's license issued to the insured, any driver in the insured's household, or any regular operator of a covered auto, other than the first or second suspension under Title 29-A M.R.S.A §2471(2), Title 29-A M.R.S.A §2472(2), or Title 28-A M.R.S.A §2052.

After the policy is in effect for more than 59 days, the insurer may cancel for loss of driving privileges by you, a relative, a rated resident, or a principal operator of a covered auto through revocation of an operator's license during the policy period or if the policy is a renewal, during its policy period or the 180 days immediately preceding its effective date.
After the policy is in effect for more than 59 days, the insurer may cancel for violation of the terms or conditions of this policy.
A postal service certificate of mailing to the named insured at the insured's last known address appearing in the insurer's records will be sufficient proof of notice of cancellation.
If the policy is cancelled, coverage will not be provided as of the effective date and time shown in the notice of cancellation.
For purposes of cancellation, the policy is neither severable nor divisible.
Any cancellation will be effective for all coverages for all persons and all vehicles.
Upon cancellation, the insured may be entitled to a premium refund.
The insurer's making or offering of a refund is not a condition of cancellation.
If the policy is cancelled, any refund due will be computed on a daily pro rata basis.
The insurer will retain a cancellation fee if the policy is cancelled at the insured's request or if cancellation is for nonpayment of premium.
A cancellation fee will be charged only during the initial policy period.

If neither the insurer nor one of its affiliates offers to renew or continue the policy, the insurer will mail notice of nonrenewal to the named insured shown on the declarations page at the last known address appearing in the insurer's records.
A postal service certificate of mailing to the named insured at the insured's last known address appearing in the insurer's records will be sufficient proof of notice of nonrenewal.
Notice of nonrenewal will be mailed more than 33 days before the end of the policy period.

If the insurer or an affiliate offers to renew or continue the policy and the insured or the insured's representative does not accept, the policy will automatically terminate at the end of the current policy period.
Failure to pay the required renewal or continuation premium when due will mean that the insured has not accepted the insurer's offer to renew.
If the insured obtains other insurance on a covered auto, any similar insurance provided by the policy will terminate as to that covered auto on the effective date of the other insurance.

If a covered auto is sold or transferred to someone other than the insured or a relative, any insurance provided by the policy will terminate as to that covered auto on the effective date of the sale or transfer.
The insurer may not be sued unless there is full compliance with all the terms of the policy.

The insurer may not be sued for payment under Part I—Liability To Others until the obligation of an insured person under Part I to pay is finally determined either by judgment after trial against that person or by written agreement of the insured person, the claimant, and the insurer.
No one will have any right to make the insurer a party to a lawsuit to determine the liability of an insured person.
If the insurer retains salvage, the insurer has no duty to preserve or otherwise retain the salvage for any purpose, including evidence for any civil or criminal proceeding.
The insurer is entitled to the rights of recovery that the insured person to whom payment was made has against another, to the extent of the insurer's payment.
The insured person may be required to sign documents related to the recovery and must do whatever else the insurer requires to help the insurer exercise those recovery rights.
The insured person must do nothing after an accident or loss to prejudice the insurer's rights of recovery.

When an insured person has been paid by the insurer and also recovers from another, the amount recovered will be held by the insured person in trust for the insurer and reimbursed to the insurer to the extent of the insurer's payment.
If the insurer is not reimbursed, the insurer may pursue recovery of that amount directly against that insured person.

When the insurer is seeking reimbursement from an insured person for payments made under Part II—Medical Payments Coverage, the insured person will be required to sign a document agreeing to reimburse the insurer out of payments made to that insured person from a liable party.

When the insurer is seeking reimbursement under Part II, the insurer is responsible for a pro rata share of the attorney fees incurred by that insured person in recovering payment from a liable party.

If an insured person recovers from another without the insurer's written consent, and such recovery prejudices the insurer's right to recover payment, the insured person's right to payment under any affected coverage will no longer exist.

If the insurer elects to exercise its rights of recovery against another, the insurer will also attempt to recover any deductible incurred by an insured person under the policy unless specifically instructed by that person not to pursue the deductible.
The insurer has no obligation to pursue recovery against another for any loss not covered by the policy.
The insurer reserves the right to compromise or settle the deductible and property damage claims against the responsible parties for less than the full amount.
The insurer reserves the right to incur reasonable expenses and attorney fees in pursuit of recovery.

If the total recovery is less than the total of the insurer's payment and the deductible, the insurer will reduce reimbursement of the deductible based on the proportion that the actual recovery bears to the total of the insurer's payment and the deductible.
A proportionate share of collection expenses and attorney fees incurred in connection with recovery efforts will also reduce reimbursement of the deductible.
The recovery provisions will be applied in accordance with state law.
If there is more than one named insured on the policy, any named insured may cancel or change the policy.
The action of one named insured will be binding on all persons provided coverage under the policy.
The bankruptcy or insolvency of an insured person will not relieve the insurer of any obligations under the policy.
